extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando won Stage 3 sprint after Quick-Step exploited crosswinds 5-8km out to create gaps, with Ewan's Orica-BikeExchange leading too early into headwind.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step forced splits at 5-8km during crosswind section before right turn, creating time gaps despite headwind finish.
Teams chose shallow-rim wheels yet still struggled with bike handling, indicating unusually severe crosswind conditions compromised positioning.
Lead-out teams caught in timing dilemma: headwind finish required late sprint but crosswind section forced early positioning commitment.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Orica-BikeExchange positioned perfectly on left to shield from right crosswinds but opened sprint too early into headwind, losing to later kick
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: neutral: attacked 400m out into headwind, immediately marked by Richeze showing limited surprise potential in these wind conditions
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Quick-Step team tactics created time gaps on exposed coastal sections, Gaviria positioned late enough to overcome headwind deficit
